title: NIH joins Trump’s $5B AI mission with biomedical research push
summary: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services announced new efforts Wednesday aligned with President Donald Trump’s artificial intelligence-driven Genesis Mission. The centerpiece of HHS’ participation is the NIH-led Bio Genesis Mission

title: House passes bill directing FDA to update animal testing rules
summary: The U.S. House of Representatives passed the FDA Modernization Act 3.0 this week, and lawmakers and animal welfare advocates are celebrating the bill as a step toward a new era in preclinical testing and drug development.

title: Royalty Pharma pens $425M deal for slice of sales on AstraZeneca’s ATTR drug
summary: Royalty Pharma has secured a stake in another transthyretin-mediated amyloidosis drug, this time paying $125 million upfront to Neurimmune for a cut of the sales of its AstraZeneca-partnered med.

title: Biotech industry leaders file brief backing FDA against ‘malicious’ mifepristone challenge
summary: A group of 61 biopharma companies, executives and investors has filed an amicus brief in support of the FDA in an ongoing case brought by Louisiana meant to remove access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: Crystalys mines $130M series B to push gout drug through phase 3
summary: Crystalys Therapeutics has raised $130 million in series B financing to advance the development of its gout drug dotinurad and prepare for a potential commercial launch.

title: Celldex ends skin disease program after failing phase 2 test
summary: A phase 2 study of Celldex’s barzolvolimab in a chronic skin disease has missed its primary endpoint, driving the biotech to end development in the indication and raising doubts about an upcoming eczema readout.

title: ‘We’re moving quickly’: Startup bags rare disease drug as Thiel-backed CEO eyes $50M fundraise
summary: A rare disease startup with a mission to repurpose proven drugs has licensed a Chinese formulation of a well-known medicine and set its sights on a $50 million fundraise.

title: ‘We’ve been looking for a novel approach’: Why Novartis CEO ended ADC holdout for $1.5B Myricx deal
summary: For years, as Big Pharma poured billions into ADCs, Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan notably stayed on the sidelines. Asked by Fierce during Novartis’ Q2 earnings call, Narasimhan explained why he is ending that holdout with the proposed $1.5 billion deal for Myricx.

title: Jupiter enters psychedelics space with $100M deal for PharmAla's MDMA drug
summary: Jupiter Neurosciences will pay $3.3 million upfront to Canada's PharmAla Biotech for U.S. rights to an MDMA drug to treat neuropsychiatric disorders.

title: Agios says adios to sickle cell program over lackluster phase 2 data
summary: A phase 2 study of Agios Pharmaceuticals’ next-generation pyruvate kinase activator has failed to support continued development in sickle cell disease, leading the company to ax an indication for the second time in two months.

title: Indian generics maker Lupin seeds US spinout Kaveri with cash, clinical-phase cancer assets
summary: Lupin has spun out two clinical-phase cancer drug candidates to create Kaveri Therapeutics, a U.S.-based startup that plans to raise cash for further development. Kaveri’s programs hit targets pursued by major drugmakers including AstraZeneca, Bayer and Bristol Myers Squibb.

title: Novartis drops phase 2 radioligand therapy, but CEO’s commitment to modality ‘unchanged’
summary: Novartis has dropped a phase 2-stage radioligand therapy after the candidate disappointed in the clinic.

title: Genetic medicine luminaries launch new center for personalized therapies
summary: A team of veteran scientists from elite institutions has come together to form a new center dedicated to the crafting of personalized genetic medicines, adding to the growing list of similar efforts in the wake of Baby KJ’s groundbreaking custom CRISPR treatment.

title: ‘There is always a first’: Why US biotech Axiom will begin its IPO journey in Hong Kong
summary: Axiom Biosciences intends to pursue an IPO on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX), followed by a co-listing on the Nasdaq—a strategy the company claims is the first U.S. biotech to use.

title: Roche taps Foundation Medicine’s chief as new North America Diagnostics lead, creates new commercial role
summary: Dan Malarek is leaving his role as chief executive of Roche’s sequencing subsidiary Foundation Medicine as he takes up the mantle as President and CEO of Roche Diagnostics North America.

title: GSK secures first lung cancer approval as FDA clears Jideytro in quick payoff from Nuvalent deal
summary: In a landmark moment for its oncology ambitions, GSK has secured FDA approval for Jideytro as the first commercial lung cancer drug in the company’s history. The milestone was made possible by GSK’s recent acquisition of Nuvalent for $10.6 billion.

title: FDA calls out Sanofi emails for giving 'misleading impression' of Beyfortus use in RSV
summary: The FDA's Office of Prescription Drug Promotion took issue with Sanofi's characterization of antibody Beyfortus as an RSV preventative, as the med is specifically indicated for RSV lower respiratory tract disease.

title: What to Expect at PODD 2026
summary: Directors Andrew Goldstein and Valerie Bowling discuss what to expect at the 2026 PODD: Partnership Opportunities in Drug Delivery conference.

title: Trump floats countdown to 200% tariffs on generic drugs imported to US
summary: While the White House focused its initial Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs on branded drugs, generics makers—who supply the vast majority of U.S. prescriptions—won’t stay in the clear for long.

title: Summit Therapeutics updates ivonescimab survival data ahead of FDA decision date
summary: Aiming to bolster ivonescimab’s case ahead of a key FDA decision, Summit Therapeutics has provided updated overall survival data from the phase 3 HARMONi trial for its PD-1xVEGF bispecific antibody.

title: Look out Ionis, here comes Arrowhead with promising hypertriglyceridemia data
summary: Less than a month after Ionis scored an FDA expansion for Tryngolza to become the first drug to specifically treat severe hypertriglyceridemia, Arrowhead has countered with head-turning data from two phase 3 trials that could set the company up for a matching nod.

title: Biotech industry leaders file brief backing FDA against ‘malicious’ mifepristone challenge
summary: A group of 61 biopharma companies, executives and investors has filed an amicus brief in support of the FDA in an ongoing case brought by Louisiana meant to remove access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: Sanofi reprimanded for disparaging Pfizer’s RSV vaccine
summary: A U.K. marketing watchdog has reprimanded Sanofi for using “a highly misleading interpretation of data” to make disparaging claims about Pfizer’s respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine.

title: Amgen agrees to $74M settlement to resolve investor lawsuit over potential $10.7B tax bill
summary: Amgen has agreed to a $74 million settlement with investors to resolve a lawsuit that alleged the company hid a $10.7 billion tax bill from the public and kept the company's share price artificially high from July of 2020 to April of 2022.

title: Sanofi continues tweaks to executive roster as new CEO Garijo settles in
summary: With former Merck KGaA CEO Belén Garijo, M.D., Ph.D., now calling the shots at Sanofi, the French drugmaker continues to refine its executive roster, albeit with fewer high-profile C-suite departures now than earlier in the year.

title: Alembic site slapped with FDA warning letter over patient consent concerns
summary: Following an inspection earlier this year that yielded a Form 483, Indian generics juggernaut Alembic Pharmaceuticals has received a harsher reprimand from the U.S. FDA.

title: Lilly, BMS aerospace partner Redwire opens new site in Indiana
summary: Aerospace technology company Redwire—which has partnered with BMS and Eli Lilly to test drug development and manufacturing in space—has opened a new 30,000-square-foot research and microgravity payload development facility in Indiana.

title: Novartis CEO brushes off oral PCSK9 competition as Leqvio drives Q2 growth
summary: Novartis' CEO remains unfazed by the threat of a newly approved oral PCSK9 inhibitor in Merck & Co.’s Lipfendra, doubling down on the long-term potential of his company's injectable cholesterol drug, Leqvio.

title: Gilead, Merck’s phase 3 data back first weekly HIV pill
summary: Detailed phase 3 results from two Islend clinical trials confirmed that a once-weekly, single-tablet combination of Merck’s islatravir and Gilead’s lenacapavir matches daily treatments like Biktarvy at maintaining HIV viral suppression

title: Novo Nordisk files lawsuit accusing rival Eli Lilly of running ‘false and misleading’ GLP-1 ads
summary: Novo Nordisk has accused Eli Lilly of running ad campaigns containing “deliberately false” messages that mislead the public about the comparative efficacy of GLP-1 medicines.

title: Vertex was sole bidder in high-premium Crinetics acquisition
summary: Analysts expect new details on the lead-up to the deal “are likely to reignite” investor scrutiny that Vertex overpaid in what’s poised to be its largest-ever transaction.

summary: The clearance is GSK’s first for a lung cancer

title: New study supports testing of Merck’s Ebola vaccine in DRC outbreak
summary: New data supports the idea that Merck’s Ebola vaccine Ervebo, licensed to target the Zaire species of ebolaviruses, could also offer some protection against another.

title: Opinion: What Argentina’s World Cup loss should teach American hospitals
summary: “Hospitals often make Argentina’s mistake: We confuse continuity with progress,” writes physician, hospital administrator, and bioethicist Basel Tarab.

title: Congo’s Ebola outbreak has killed over 1,000 people
summary: At least 999 people have died from the Ebola outbreak in Congo, a grim toll in what’s already been declared the fastest Ebola outbreak in history.

title: STAT+: ‘Cochlear implant for blindness’ debuts in Europe, could launch in U.S. in 2027
summary: Science Corporation announced the European launch of its retinal implant for patients with age-related macular degeneration.

title: STAT+: Federal health quality research grants worth $109 million ended early
summary: At least 78 AHRQ grants worth $200 million have been affected by "non-award" notices; it's estimated those grants had $109.2 million in funding remaining.

title: Opinion: STAT+: I’m a cardiologist. I’m not sure the new cholesterol drug enlicitide is a game-changer
summary: “In truth, I don’t anticipate prescribing patterns changing immediately,” cardiologist Vishal Khetpal writes of the new cholesterol drug Lipfendra.

title: Opinion: STAT+: Hospitals’ AI may be drifting. Who’s watching?
summary: Senior leaders must treat AI governance as their own daily job, not something the technology committee handles quarterly, write Peter Pronovost, Justin Norden, and Kedar Mate.

title: As Maui recovers from devastating fires, a study offers vision for new rural health model
summary: A study of the long-term health impact of the Lahaina wildfires is inspiring a new model for disaster recovery and support.

title: STAT+: More workers hate their jobs, but keep them for the health insurance
summary: A new survey reveals 24% of U.S. workers stay in unwanted jobs to keep health insurance. Experts call it 'job lock.'
